title: Cloudflare Just Hit the AI-First Reset Button: Susquehanna Hikes Price Target to $200
text: Susquehanna analyst Shyam Patil raised his price target on Cloudflare to $200 from $190 while maintaining a Neutral rating. The analyst cited the company's strategic repositioning toward an AI-first operating model as a significant catalyst for long-term value creation. Patil noted that the 20% workforce reduction, while controversial in the short term, positions Cloudflare to operate with greater efficiency and profitability margins as the company scales AI-driven services. The analyst highlighted the company's demonstrated capability to generate significant revenue growth (34% YoY in Q1 2026) while simultaneously planning to improve operating leverage through the restructuring. Patil's upgrade reflects growing analyst confidence that the AI-driven transformation will yield improved financial outcomes over the next 2-3 years. The Susquehanna analyst emphasizes that the market has been underestimating Cloudflare's potential in the enterprise AI infrastructure market. The price target increase suggests meaningful upside from the April lows, though Patil's Neutral rating indicates a balanced risk-reward profile at current levels.
